# Case Study: Colon Cancer Survival

This chapter runs the whole pipeline on the `colon` trial that ships with the
`survival` package, so it needs no download. The exposure of interest is
`node4` (more than four positive lymph nodes) on the death endpoint.

```r
library(forkflow)
library(survival)
library(broom)
library(furrr)
plan(multisession)

d <- colon |> dplyr::filter(etype == 2) |> tidyr::drop_na()

specs <- adjuster_sets(
  c("age", "sex", "obstruct", "perfor", "adhere", "differ", "surg")
)

results <- fit_multiverse(specs, function(spec) {
  f <- reformulate(c("node4", spec$adjusters[[1]]), "Surv(time, status)")
  coxph(f, data = d) |> tidy(conf.int = TRUE) |>
    dplyr::filter(term == "node4")
}, .parallel = TRUE, .seed = 2026)

voe(results)
spec_curve(results)
voe_volcano(results, colour = n_adjusters)
report_multiverse(results, dir = "outputs")
```

Reading the outputs: the specification curve shows where the mass of estimates
sits and how much crosses the null; the volcano shows how the hazard ratio for
`node4` vibrates as covariates enter and leave; and `voe()` reduces the spread
to a relative hazard ratio and a Janus fraction.
